text:
Rain on a roof at night. The rhythm is irregular but patterned enough to feel deliberate. Each drop lands distinctly, then blends into the continuous wash of the whole. It creates a kind of white noise that is not actually white noise — it has texture, variation, small surprises.

The reason it is pleasing is structural. The sound signals enclosure: you are inside, sheltered, dry. The external world is doing something energetic while you are still. There is a contrast built into the experience that the brain reads as comfort.

It also requires nothing from you. You do not need to interpret it or respond to it. It simply continues, self-sustaining, indifferent to your attention. That quality — something happening that does not need you — is genuinely restful.

Most pleasant sounds share this: they ask nothing back.
